package repository

import (
	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"gorm.io/gorm"
)

// Anti-pattern: Repository method accepts *gin.Context instead of context.Context.
// This couples the data layer to the HTTP transport layer.

type User struct {
	ID   string `gorm:"column:id"`
	Name string `gorm:"column:name"`
}

type UserRepository struct {
	db *gorm.DB
}

// Find accepts gin.Context - this is the anti-pattern.
// Repositories should accept context.Context, not framework-specific context types.
func (r *UserRepository) Find(c *gin.Context, id string) (*User, error) {
	tenantID := c.GetHeader("X-Tenant-ID")
	var user User
	err := r.db.Where("id = ? AND tenant_id = ?", id, tenantID).First(&user).Error
	return &user, err
}

// List also accepts gin.Context and extracts query params directly.
func (r *UserRepository) List(c *gin.Context) ([]User, error) {
	page := c.DefaultQuery("page", "1")
	limit := c.DefaultQuery("limit", "10")
	var users []User
	err := r.db.Offset((pageToInt(page) - 1) * limitToInt(limit)).Limit(limitToInt(limit)).Find(&users).Error
	return users, err
}

func pageToInt(s string) int {
	return 1
}

func limitToInt(s string) int {
	return 10
}
